commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Feishu VC meeting invite event handling, synthetic inbound DM dispatch with join prompt, dedupe and reply routing tests, and Feishu docs for the new event subscription.

PR surface: Source +394, Tests +505, Docs +1. Total +900 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking finding: source inspection shows normal Feishu DMs apply configured/runtime conversation bindings, while the new VC invite handler resolves only a bare direct route before recording and dispatch. I did not rerun a live Feishu invite.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Queue repair for the concrete routing defect; maintainers can still make the final product-boundary decision after the fix.

target.title: feat(feishu): handle VC meeting invites
target.body:
## Summary

- Register Feishu `vc.bot.meeting_invited_v1` in the bundled `feishu` monitor dispatcher.
- Convert valid VC meeting invite events into synthetic inbound Feishu direct-message turns from the inviter, reusing the existing DM pairing, allowlist, dynamic agent creation, session recording, and reply-dispatch path.
- Ask the agent to join immediately by meeting number, with instructions to pass `call_id` only when the installed join tool exposes that parameter.
- Route follow-up replies to the real Feishu inviter target (`user:<open_id>`), not the synthetic meeting route, so the normal `message` tool and final replies stay on the supported Feishu DM channel.
- Document the required `vc.bot.meeting_invited_v1` event subscription for meeting invite auto-join.

## Linked context

This PR is based on the Feishu VC meeting invite behavior tested from live bot invitations and the sibling Feishu invite flow. There is no linked issue.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Feishu VC bot meeting invite events now reach OpenClaw through the bundled `feishu` plugin and trigger an immediate meeting-join turn while preserving the normal Feishu reply/session route back to the inviter.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway using the linked bundled `extensions/feishu` plugin, with a live Feishu bot and live VC meeting invitations.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built and installed the current OpenClaw branch locally, verified the gateway was loading `openclaw` rather than the older external `openclaw-lark` plugin, restarted the gateway after rebuilding, sent live Feishu VC meeting invitations to the bot, inspected gateway/runtime logs and agent tool invocations, then reran the focused validation commands listed below.

Evidence after fix: Runtime logs showed `feishu[default]: vc meeting invited, dispatching synthetic inbound` for live meeting invites. The join path invoked `lark-cli vc +meeting-join --meeting-number ... --format json`; one invalid meeting returned Feishu code `121005` (`meeting not exist`), and a later live invite joined successfully with a meeting number, meeting id, start time, and topic returned by Feishu. Focused tests assert the finalized inbound context uses `To` and `OriginatingTo` as `user:<inviter open_id>`, creates a Feishu reply dispatcher for that user target, and does not call the removed direct acknowledgement send path for dispatched invites.

Observed result after fix: The live Feishu invite event was accepted by the `feishu` plugin, converted into a synthetic inbound turn, joined the meeting by meeting number, and kept follow-up agent replies on the inviter's standard Feishu direct-message route instead of `vc-meeting:<meeting_no>`.

What was not tested: I did not attach screenshots or sanitized transcript artifacts to the PR. The live validation used local gateway logs and Feishu runtime/tool output rather than a published artifact.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: The real-environment proof depends on a live Feishu bot, live meeting invite, and local gateway configuration. The PR therefore includes focused regression tests for the dispatch, dedupe, reply target, pairing-blocked, and compatibility branches so the behavior remains reviewable without access to that live Feishu environment.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/monitor.vc-meeting-invited-handler.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
- GitHub CI on `51d83d72bfae18aeeb9d704a1a3a169fd7619e1a`: `check-lint`, `check-additional-extension-bundled`, `check-test-types`, `check-prod-types`, `check-guards`, `check-additional-extension-package-boundary`, and `Real behavior proof` completed successfully.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior: yes. A Feishu VC invite to the bot can now create an inbound turn that attempts to join the meeting.
- Configuration/operator action: yes. Feishu apps must subscribe to `vc.bot.meeting_invited_v1`; the docs were updated with that event.
- Message delivery/session routing: covered. Invite-triggered replies use the real inviter DM target (`user:<open_id>`) and avoid persisting a synthetic `vc-meeting:*` last-route target.
- Tool compatibility: covered. The prompt instructs the agent to use `call_id` only when the available join tool supports it, keeping older `lark-cli` versions usable.
- Security/privacy: no new credentials or persisted secrets. The handler keeps invite context limited to the fields needed for joining and reply routing.
- Rollback: disable the Feishu event subscription or the bundled `feishu` channel/plugin to stop invite-triggered turns.

## Current review state

- Code comments: no open inline review comments were present when this description was refreshed.
- Previously raised `session-state` and `message-delivery` concerns were addressed by routing both `To` and `OriginatingTo` to `user:<inviter open_id>` and removing the one-off direct acknowledgement send for dispatched invites.
- Current known gap: PR labels still include stale ClawSweeper review labels from a failed/timed-out review run; a fresh ClawSweeper review should be requested after this description refresh if maintainers want those labels recalculated.
- Next reviewer focus: confirm the owner-boundary choice of reusing the existing Feishu inbound/reply dispatcher path for VC invite turns, and verify the live proof is sufficient for merge.
target.author: ren1104

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

- `node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
- GitHub CI on `51d83d72bfae18aeeb9d704a1a3a169fd7619e1a`: `check-lint`, `check-additional-extension-bundled`, `check-test-types`, `check-prod-types`, `check-guards`, `check-additional-extension-package-boundary`, and `Real behavior proof` completed successfully.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior: yes. A Feishu VC invite to the bot can now create an inbound turn that attempts to join the meeting.
- Configuration/operator action: yes. Feishu apps must subscribe to `vc.bot.meeting_invited_v1`; the docs were updated with that event.
- Message delivery/session routing: covered. Invite-triggered replies use the real inviter DM target (`user:<open_id>`) and avoid persisting a synthetic `vc-meeting:*` last-route target.
- Tool compatibility: covered. The prompt instructs the agent to use `call_id` only when the available join tool supports it, keeping older `lark-cli` versions usable.
- Security/privacy: no new credentials or persisted secrets. The handler keeps invite context limited to the fields needed for joining and reply routing.
- Rollback: disable the Feishu event subscription or the bundled `feishu` channel/plugin to stop invite-triggered turns.

## Current review state

- Code comments: no open inline review comments were present when this description was refreshed.
- Previously raised `session-state` and `message-delivery` concerns were addressed by routing both `To` and `OriginatingTo` to `user:<inviter open_id>` and removing the one-off direct acknowledgement send for dispatched invites.

- Next reviewer focus: confirm the owner-boundary choice of reusing the existing Feishu inbound/reply dispatcher path for VC invite turns, and verify the live proof is sufficient for merge.

commands.author: Ren1104

target.title: feat(feishu): handle VC meeting invites
target.body:
## Summary

- Register Feishu `vc.bot.meeting_invited_v1` in the bundled `feishu` monitor dispatcher.
- Convert valid VC meeting invite events into synthetic inbound Feishu direct-message turns from the inviter, reusing the existing DM pairing, allowlist, dynamic agent creation, session recording, and reply-dispatch path.
- Ask the agent to join immediately by meeting number, with instructions to pass `call_id` only when the installed join tool exposes that parameter.
- Route follow-up replies to the real Feishu inviter target (`user:<open_id>`), not the synthetic meeting route, so the normal `message` tool and final replies stay on the supported Feishu DM channel.
- Document the required `vc.bot.meeting_invited_v1` event subscription for meeting invite auto-join.

## Linked context

This PR is based on the Feishu VC meeting invite behavior tested from live bot invitations and the sibling Feishu invite flow. There is no linked issue.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Feishu VC bot meeting invite events now reach OpenClaw through the bundled `feishu` plugin and trigger an immediate meeting-join turn while preserving the normal Feishu reply/session route back to the inviter.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway using the linked bundled `extensions/feishu` plugin, with a live Feishu bot and live VC meeting invitations.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built and installed the current OpenClaw branch locally, verified the gateway was loading `openclaw` rather than the older external `openclaw-lark` plugin, restarted the gateway after rebuilding, sent live Feishu VC meeting invitations to the bot, inspected gateway/runtime logs and agent tool invocations, then reran the focused validation commands listed below.

Evidence after fix: Runtime logs showed `feishu[default]: vc meeting invited, dispatching synthetic inbound` for live meeting invites. The join path invoked `lark-cli vc +meeting-join --meeting-number ... --format json`; one invalid meeting returned Feishu code `121005` (`meeting not exist`), and a later live invite joined successfully with a meeting number, meeting id, start time, and topic returned by Feishu. Focused tests assert the finalized inbound context uses `To` and `OriginatingTo` as `user:<inviter open_id>`, creates a Feishu reply dispatcher for that user target, and does not call the removed direct acknowledgement send path for dispatched invites.

Observed result after fix: The live Feishu invite event was accepted by the `feishu` plugin, converted into a synthetic inbound turn, joined the meeting by meeting number, and kept follow-up agent replies on the inviter's standard Feishu direct-message route instead of `vc-meeting:<meeting_no>`.

What was not tested: I did not attach screenshots or sanitized transcript artifacts to the PR. The live validation used local gateway logs and Feishu runtime/tool output rather than a published artifact.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: The real-environment proof depends on a live Feishu bot, live meeting invite, and local gateway configuration. The PR therefore includes focused regression tests for the dispatch, dedupe, reply target, pairing-blocked, and compatibility branches so the behavior remains reviewable without access to that live Feishu environment.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/monitor.vc-meeting-invited-handler.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
- GitHub CI on `51d83d72bfae18aeeb9d704a1a3a169fd7619e1a`: `check-lint`, `check-additional-extension-bundled`, `check-test-types`, `check-prod-types`, `check-guards`, `check-additional-extension-package-boundary`, and `Real behavior proof` completed successfully.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior: yes. A Feishu VC invite to the bot can now create an inbound turn that attempts to join the meeting.
- Configuration/operator action: yes. Feishu apps must subscribe to `vc.bot.meeting_invited_v1`; the docs were updated with that event.
- Message delivery/session routing: covered. Invite-triggered replies use the real inviter DM target (`user:<open_id>`) and avoid persisting a synthetic `vc-meeting:*` last-route target.
- Tool compatibility: covered. The prompt instructs the agent to use `call_id` only when the available join tool supports it, keeping older `lark-cli` versions usable.
- Security/privacy: no new credentials or persisted secrets. The handler keeps invite context limited to the fields needed for joining and reply routing.
- Rollback: disable the Feishu event subscription or the bundled `feishu` channel/plugin to stop invite-triggered turns.

## Current review state

- Code comments: no open inline review comments were present when this description was refreshed.
- Previously raised `session-state` and `message-delivery` concerns were addressed by routing both `To` and `OriginatingTo` to `user:<inviter open_id>` and removing the one-off direct acknowledgement send for dispatched invites.
- Current known gap: PR labels still include stale ClawSweeper review labels from a failed/timed-out review run; a fresh ClawSweeper review should be requested after this description refresh if maintainers want those labels recalculated.
- Next reviewer focus: confirm the owner-boundary choice of reusing the existing Feishu inbound/reply dispatcher path for VC invite turns, and verify the live proof is sufficient for merge.
